headline: Penang Flood Retention Ponds Converted to 6 Housing Projects
context: Penang Island City Council records show 6 designated flood retention ponds were rezoned for housing development between 2018 and 2024, reducing retention capacity by 2.4 million cubic metres.

cards.big: Penang's flood retention ponds are designed to hold 8.2 million cubic metres.
cards.sub: 6 ponds were rezoned for housing. Retention capacity fell 29%. Penang's 2024 floods were the worst in 18 years.

cards.big: Each converted pond site generated RM120-280M in property development.
cards.sub: Developers pay a 'replacement contribution' averaging RM4.2M — less than 3% of project value.

cards.big: No replacement ponds were built for 4 of the 6 conversions.
cards.sub: The contribution was paid but the replacement infrastructure was never constructed.

cards.big: 2024 flood damage in affected areas totalled RM620M.
cards.sub: Insurance claims cited 'inadequate drainage infrastructure' — the same ponds that were converted.

cards.big: Selling flood ponds for RM4.2M each, then paying RM620M in flood damage. The arithmetic is disastrous.
Flood retention infrastructure must be legally unconvertible. A replacement contribution that is never replaced is a development subsidy funded by future flood victims.
